essence: Uncovers truth through investigation, intuition, and a disciplined commitment to justice.

identity.title: Police Detective
identity.summary: Investigates crimes, gathers evidence, interviews witnesses and suspects, and builds cases that support prosecution and protect the public.

salary.notes: Specialist units (CID, homicide, cybercrime) and supervisory roles can exceed £60k–£70k+. Overtime and on‑call duties can increase earnings.

qualifications: Strong Understanding of Criminal Law and Procedure
Experience in Evidence Handling and Interview Techniques

lifestyle.description: Detectives work in a demanding, unpredictable environment where cases can shift rapidly and require long hours of focused investigation. Days involve interviewing witnesses, analysing evidence, writing reports, attending crime scenes, and collaborating with forensic teams. The emotional weight can be significant — detectives encounter trauma, conflict, and human complexity regularly. The work requires patience, intuition, and the ability to see patterns others miss. While the pressure is high, the sense of purpose is strong: solving crimes, protecting victims, and bringing clarity to difficult situations. This lifestyle suits individuals who are analytical, resilient, observant, and motivated by justice.

habits: Reviews case files regularly for new angles
